summary: The Sprint 2 code is generally well-structured and follows project conventions. Tests are comprehensive, properly organized into classes, use descriptive names, and cover edge cases. The use of logging instead of print statements is consistent throughout. Type annotations are present on all public functions. Alembic setup follows async patterns correctly. However, several maintainability concerns were found: text_to_sql.py mixes SQL generation, SQL validation, SQL execution, and answer generation in a single file; the SYSTEM_PROMPT is a module-level f-string eagerly evaluated at import time making it hard to test with different schemas; the dynamic SQL construction in ingest_batting.py is fragile and hard to read; and config.py defaults embed connection strings that obscure intent. The test coverage strategy is solid with 45 tests for text_to_sql.py demonstrating commitment to TDD.

findings.title: text_to_sql.py mixes four concerns in one file violates SRP
findings.detail: text_to_sql.py contains: SQL generation via LLM (generate_sql), SQL security validation (_validate_select_only), SQL execution with timeout (execute_sql), and natural language answer generation (generate_answer). These are four distinct responsibilities. As the project grows, this file will become large and hard to navigate. Mixing I/O-bound LLM calls and pure validation logic in the same module also complicates unit testing.
findings.fix: Extract into focused modules: app/sql_validator.py (pure validation functions), app/llm_client.py (Anthropic client wrapper), and keep app/text_to_sql.py as a thin orchestration layer under 80 lines.

findings.title: SYSTEM_PROMPT eagerly evaluated as f-string at module import
findings.detail: SYSTEM_PROMPT is a module-level f-string baked in at import time. The prompt cannot be varied per-request or per-environment; tests that want a simplified schema must patch the entire string; and future schema-aware prompt generation requires refactoring.
findings.fix: Convert to a function: def _build_system_prompt(schema: str = DB_SCHEMA) -> str returning the formatted string. Call it lazily when needed. This makes testing trivial and enables future schema injection.

findings.title: ingest_batting.py dynamic SQL construction is brittle and unreadable
findings.detail: The load() function builds the upsert SQL by string-joining DataFrame column names. This produces SQL that is impossible to read in isolation. There is no logging of the generated SQL before execution, making debugging failed upserts difficult.
findings.fix: Add logger.debug logging of the generated upsert SQL before conn.execute(). Extract the SQL-building logic into a _build_upsert_sql(table, columns, conflict_cols) helper function with a docstring showing an example output.

findings.title: config.py sensitive default values make misconfiguration silent
findings.detail: The Settings class has database_url defaulting to a full connection string with embedded password changeme, and api key fields defaulting to empty string. A developer who forgets to set environment variables gets no startup warning and only discovers the problem when an API call fails at runtime.
findings.fix: Use pydantic-settings model_validator to emit warnings at startup when API keys are missing. For database_url, remove the default to force explicit configuration, or add a startup check that detects the default changeme password and raises a ValueError.

findings.title: Alembic initial migration missing FK constraints for referential integrity
findings.detail: The batting_stats and pitching_stats tables have player_mlb_id columns that logically reference players.mlb_id, but no ForeignKeyConstraint is defined in the migration. Without FK constraints, the DB allows orphaned stat rows causing silent data integrity issues.
findings.fix: Add FK constraints in the migration: sa.ForeignKeyConstraint(['player_mlb_id'], ['players.mlb_id'], name='fk_batting_stats_player', ondelete='CASCADE'). Do the same for pitching_stats.

findings.title: Private helper functions directly imported in tests tight coupling to internals
findings.detail: Functions like _strip_sql_fence and _validate_select_only are underscore-prefixed (private) but directly imported in test_text_to_sql.py. This couples the test suite to implementation details. If these functions are moved during refactoring, tests break.
findings.fix: Either add __all__ = ['generate_sql', 'execute_sql', 'generate_answer'] to declare the public API, or add a comment documenting that _validate_select_only is intentionally tested directly as a security-critical component.

findings.title: conftest.py mock_db fixture unused dead code
findings.detail: The mock_db fixture is defined in conftest.py but test_text_to_sql.py uses a local _make_mock_session() helper instead. The mock_db fixture is therefore unused, creating dead code that confuses future developers about which pattern to follow.
findings.fix: Either remove the unused mock_db fixture from conftest.py, or update it to be flexible enough for the two-call side_effect pattern needed for SET LOCAL statement_timeout and document why.
